---
title: "Microclimate.jl"
subtitle: "Physics-based microclimates from points to landscapes"
author: "Rafael Schouten — Montpellier 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    width: 1280
    height: 800
    margin: 0.05
    code-line-numbers: false
    highlight-style: github
---

## Why microclimate?

Ecologists increasingly need physics-based microclimate predictions over large areas.

::: {.incremental}
- Bioclim layers describe **macro**climate at coarse weather-grid scales — 2 m air, monthly means.
- Most organisms — soil fauna, lizards on rocks, seedlings under canopy — live in the **micro**climate that terrain and surface properties create at sub-metre, sub-hour scales.
- Predicting it requires downscaling weather + DEM + land-cover through coupled physics: radiation, soil heat & water, boundary-layer profiles.
:::

::: {.fragment}
And we need a lot of it: every pixel of a continent, every day of a decade, sometimes both.
:::

---

## The three tensions

::: {.incremental}
1. **Performance**: per-pixel solves over continental grids must be fast.
2. **Flexibility**: different climate regimes need different physics formulations — vapour pressure over ice, snow phase transitions, evaporation parameterisations.
3. **Composability**: new methods from the literature should drop in without forking the package.
:::

::: {.fragment}
Existing tools (NicheMapR, microclima, …) bundle these decisions. You get a fixed combination, or you fork Fortran.
:::

---

## A different approach

::: {.incremental}
- **Declarative**: a `MicroMapModel` describes *what* to run — data sources, soil model, snow model, lapse-rate model. Reusable across sites.
- **Modular**: every physics component is a Julia type with a multiple-dispatch interface. Add your own without editing the package.
- **Composable**: weather sources, surface-property datasets, output layers — all plug in by passing a type.
- **Unitful**: temperatures, pressures, lengths, fluxes all carry `Unitful` units. Bad conversions are compile errors.
:::

---

## Modularity by example: vapour pressure

`FluidProperties.jl` provides several `vapour_pressure` formulations. Each is a struct; pick one by passing it.

```julia
using FluidProperties, Unitful

vapour_pressure(GoffGratch(), 273.15u"K")     # ≈ 611 Pa
vapour_pressure(Teten(),       273.15u"K")    # different formula, same interface
vapour_pressure(Huang(),       273.15u"K")    # ditto
```

::: {.fragment}
Adding a new formulation is one method definition. No fork required, no recompile of any
upstream package — write the method in your script.
:::

```julia
struct MyFormulation <: VapourPressureEquation end
FluidProperties.vapour_pressure(::MyFormulation, T) = ...
```

---

## Microclimate.jl

A Julia rewrite of the physics behind [NicheMapR](https://github.com/mrke/NicheMapR) (Kearney & Porter 2017).

::: {.incremental}
- 1-D coupled soil-energy + atmospheric-profile model.
- Heat & water transport in the soil column, Monin–Obukhov boundary layer above.
- Optional `SnowModel` with consecutive-day accumulation/melt.
- Hourly air, soil, humidity, wind at user-chosen heights and depths.
- Decoupled, composable Julia packages instead of monolithic Fortran.
:::

---

## The package ecosystem

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 14
%%| fig-height: 6
flowchart LR
  subgraph data["Data"]
    direction TB
    RDS[RasterDataSources.jl]
    RAS[Rasters.jl]
  end

  subgraph forcing["Forcing"]
    direction TB
    SOL[SolarRadiation.jl]
    FLU[FluidProperties.jl]
    GEO[Geomorphometry.jl]
  end

  subgraph core["Physics core"]
    direction TB
    MIC[Microclimate.jl]
    MP[ModelParameters.jl]
    UF[Unitful.jl]
    SCI[SciML / OrdinaryDiffEq]
  end

  subgraph scale["Landscape"]
    BPG[MicroclimateMapper.jl]
  end

  subgraph eco["Ecology models"]
    direction TB
    BEH[BiophysicalBehaviour.jl]
    HEX[HeatExchange.jl]
    DEB[DynamicEnergyBudgets.jl]
    SDM["Any SDM"]
  end

  data --> forcing
  data --> scale
  forcing --> core
  MP --> core
  UF --> core
  SCI --> core
  core --> scale
  core --> eco
  scale --> eco

  classDef ext fill:#eef,stroke:#557
  classDef core fill:#fee,stroke:#a44
  classDef scale fill:#efe,stroke:#4a4
  class RDS,RAS,SOL,FLU,GEO,MP,UF,SCI ext
  class MIC core
  class BPG scale
```

::: {.fragment}
Each box is an independent registered Julia package. Swap one out, the rest still work.
:::

---

## A microclimate at a few points

One declarative `MicroMapModel` describes *what* to run — data sources, soil model, snow model.
A `MicroPointsProblem` pairs it with the points and the year range.

```julia
using MicroclimateMapper, Unitful
using Rasters: Ti, lookup

depths  = [0.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0, 15.0, 20.0, 30.0, 50.0, 100.0, 200.0]u"cm"
heights = [0.01, 2.0]u"m"

inner = MicroModel(;
    days    = collect(1:365),                                # daily timesteps
    depths, heights,
    soil_profile          = example_soil_profile(depths),
    soil_properties_model = example_soil_properties_model(),
    soil_hydraulic_model  = example_soil_hydraulic_model(depths),
    snow_model            = SnowModel(),
    config                = MicroConfig(; time_mode = ConsecutiveDayMode()),
)

model = MicroMapModel(;
    micro_model             = inner,
    dem_source              = SRTM,
    weather_source          = NCEP{SurfaceGauss},        # daily reanalysis
    surface_albedo_source   = 0.15,
    roughness_height_source = 0.004u"m",
)

points = [(3.520, 44.143), (3.582, 44.115), (3.581, 44.122)]   # Cévennes
problem = MicroPointsProblem(;
    model, points, years = 2010:2010,
    init = (; soil_moisture = fill(0.25, length(depths))),
)

output = solve(problem)   # RasterStack: (Dim{:point}, Ti, depth | height)
```

::: {.fragment}
Same NCEP cell drives all three points — terrain lapse-correction does the rest.
:::

---

## Winter snowpack — Mont Aigoual, 2010

![](figures/point_snow_depth.png){fig-align="center" width="85%"}

Daily NCEP forcing in `ConsecutiveDayMode`: snow accumulates and melts across the winter.
Same weather cell, three elevations, ≈ 50 cm difference in peak snowpack.

---

## A representative winter day

![](figures/point_temperatures.png){fig-align="center" width="85%"}

Hourly soil and air temperatures at each site for one mid-January day.
Same forcing, same physics — terrain alone splits the outcomes.

---

## Scaling up: `MicroMapProblem`

Same `MicroMapModel`, paired with a rectangular extent instead of points.

```julia
using MicroclimateMapper, Rasters
using Rasters.Extents: Extent

area = Extent(X = (3.554, 3.608), Y = (44.095, 44.149))   # Mont Aigoual

problem = MicroMapProblem(;
    model, area, years = 2000:2000,
    template = SRTM,            # run on the SRTM grid (~64 × 64 cells)
)
output  = solve(problem)        # RasterStack: (X, Y, Ti, depth | height)
```

::: {.fragment}
- One `solve` call. Threading over pixels happens internally.
- Weather resampled onto the `template` grid; terrain (slope, aspect, horizon angles)
  computed from the DEM at native resolution and aggregated onto the template.
- Same `output_layers` schema as the point case — heatmap-ready Rasters.
:::

---

## Mont Aigoual, Cévennes — July 2000

Soil-surface temperature, 6 hours of the diurnal cycle.

![](figures/grid_soil_temperature_surface.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

::: {.fragment}
North-facing slopes stay cool past noon. South-facing rocks reach 50 °C.
:::

---

## Air temperature at 1 cm — animated

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](figures/grid_air_temperature_1cm.gif){fig-align="center"}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
- TerraClimate forcing at native resolution
- 24 hourly snapshots
- Threaded across pixels
- Same `solve(problem)` call as the point case
- ~minutes on a laptop
:::

:::

---

## Air at 2 m vs soil surface

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Air, 2 m** — what TerraClimate gives you.

![](figures/grid_air_temperature_2m.png)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Soil surface** — what a lizard feels.

![](figures/grid_soil_temperature_surface.png)
:::

:::

The map you need depends on the organism. Both come from the same model run.

---

## Snow melting on a spring day

Same model + extent, just `snow_model = SnowModel()` and an initial 30 cm snowpack.

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="55%"}
![](figures/grid_snow_depth.gif){fig-align="center"}
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
```julia
snow_problem = MicroMapProblem(;
    model = build_model(SnowModel()),
    area, years = 2000:2000,
    init = (; snow_depth = 30.0u"cm"),
)
solve(snow_problem)
```

- South-facing slopes melt out by mid-afternoon
- North aspects + summit keep the snowpack
- Aspect + elevation alone, no extra plumbing
:::

:::

---

## Swap the weather source — NCEP daily, full year

Same `MicroMapModel`, one field changed: `weather_source = NCEP{SurfaceGauss}`.
Daily reanalysis + `ConsecutiveDayMode` carries snow state day-to-day across 2010.

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="55%"}
![](figures/grid_snow_depth_yearly.gif){fig-align="center"}
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
```julia
yearly_problem = MicroMapProblem(;
    model = MicroMapModel(;
        micro_model = MicroModel(;
            depths, heights, snow_model = SnowModel(),
            config = MicroConfig(;
                time_mode = ConsecutiveDayMode(spinup_first_day = true),
            ),
            # ...soil settings as before
        ),
        dem_source              = SRTM,
        weather_source          = NCEP{SurfaceGauss},   # was TerraClimate
        surface_albedo_source   = 0.15,
        roughness_height_source = 0.004u"m",
    ),
    area, years = 2010:2010, template = SRTM,
    init = (; snow_depth = 0.0u"cm"),
)
solve(yearly_problem)
```

- Snowpack grows from zero through real daily snowfall events
- Terrain alone splits accumulation and melt across the grid
- One-line source switch — same physics, same `solve`
:::

:::

---

## What you put on top is up to you

The `RasterStack` from `solve(problem)` is forcing for *any* organism-level model:

::: {.incremental}
- **Heat budgets** — operative temperature, ectotherm activity windows (`HeatExchange.jl`, `BiophysicalBehaviour.jl`)
- **Dynamic Energy Budget** — growth, reproduction, survival (`DynamicEnergyBudgets.jl`)
- **Species distribution models** — replace bioclim layers with mechanistic ones; correlative or process-based, your choice
- **Your model** — read from a `RasterStack`, no FFI, no NetCDF gymnastics
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Microclimate is a service, not a destination.**
:::

---

## Status & roadmap

::: {.incremental}
- Microclimate.jl — validated against NicheMapR's monthly outputs at point scale
- MicroclimateMapper.jl — declarative `MicroMapModel` + `MicroMapProblem` / `MicroPointsProblem` API
- Weather sources: NCEP daily, AWAP daily, ERA5 hourly, TerraClimate / CHELSA / WorldClim monthly
- Snow model with consecutive-day accumulation/melt
- Cold-air drainage, urban canopies — in progress
- Open source, MIT, contributions welcome
:::

---

## Thanks

::: {.incremental}
- **Mike Kearney** (Melbourne) — NicheMapR, biophysical ecology vision
- BiophysicalEcology contributors
- Rasters.jl, SciML, JuliaGeo ecosystems
:::

::: {.fragment}

**Code & slides**: [github.com/BiophysicalEcology](https://github.com/BiophysicalEcology)

**This talk**: pre-rendered, source open, every figure rebuilt in CI.

:::
